finding: Dimensional trait partitions into multiple discrete subgroups along agentic vs antagonistic axes; within moderate trait levels, one subgroup expresses only approach dynamics while another expresses both approach and avoidance, showing opposing correlations with adjustment outcomes despite equivalent trait magnitude.

Deficit in emotional word encoding varies by emotional valence and is modulated by contextual emotional priming; specifically, incongruent prime-word pairings impair retrieval while congruent pairings partially compensate for the processing deficit.

Stress-buffering effect of outlook disposition is partly explained by self-reported availability of support rather than actual support provision; disposition shapes perception of support more than it shapes support provision.

Unregistered competitive pressure increases formalized firm innovation; this effect attenuates when registered competition is high, alternative informal adaptation is available, or decision-makers expect favorable institutional change.

Frame does not change aggregate allocation but flips the distribution of behavioral types by gender; taking frame makes males more selfish and females more egalitarian relative to giving frame, canceling aggregate effect.

Subjects invest substantially in public infrastructure despite sequential access asymmetry; coordination improves outcomes over time, but downstream participants reduce future investment if prior earnings were inequitably distributed, creating feedback cycle.
